response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates relevant geospatial expertise and distributed systems thinking from high-throughput infrastructure work, there is a critical technical gap. Their only commercial C++ experience was 6 months at National Instruments over a decade ago; the past 6+ years have focused exclusively on TypeScript and web technologies. For a senior individual contributor role on critical production routing infrastructure requiring modern C++ as the primary language, this recency and depth gap is disqualifying. Additionally, there is no navigation or routing systems domain experience.
strengths: Geospatial domain knowledge from building and maintaining GISCollective, an event-driven platform deployed on Kubernetes for real-world conservation use cases
Distributed systems and high-performance architecture understanding from Dunnhumby's micro-services infrastructure handling millions of requests/second with async I/O and caching strategies
Proven ability to rapidly master new languages and technical domains across embedded systems, web, mobile, and backend work
concerns: Minimal C++ experience limited to 6 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015; no professional C++ work in over a decade
Recent 6+ year career trajectory focused entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies, moving away from systems programming rather than toward it
No navigation or routing systems experience; prior geospatial work was mapping and data collection, not routing optimization or tile generation
